headline: Swanky Scoops opening a Warehouse District scoop shop
paragraphs: The Blade reported (May 16) that Alissa Vieira's artisanal ice cream business, Swanky Scoops, is opening a brick-and-mortar shop in the Warehouse District — graduating from its production-only kitchen at 130 10th Street into a destination retail space with more square footage, more seating, expanded hours, and event programming.
why_it_matters: Small ticket on its own, but it lands in the right place at the right time — the Warehouse District is being reframed as a daytime/family-traffic destination through Huron Yards' grocery and restaurant additions and the upcoming beer garden. A scoop shop turns "people pass through here for ballgames" into "people walk here on weekends." Track which block they sign in — Huron Yards-adjacent reinforces the Erie Street cluster; Hensville-adjacent diversifies the entertainment-zone tenant mix.
search_terms: Swanky Scoops Warehouse District Toledo Vieira ice cream opening
